description_long: This object is a cylindrical bollard constructed to resemble stacked stone blocks, with a rough, weathered surface and visible mortar lines. The exterior is predominantly a brownish-tan color, with irregular dark lines simulating the joints between the stone segments. Subtle greenish patches suggest moss or weathering, adding to its aged, urban appearance. The top of the bollard is flat and circular, matching the stone texture of the sides. The structure appears solid and robust, likely intended to prevent vehicle access or demarcate pedestrian zones. There are no visible openings, connectors, or reflective markings, and the base is flush with the ground.
description: A cylindrical, stone-textured bollard with simulated block joints and weathered, mossy patches. The surface is rough and aged, with a flat top and no visible connectors.
description_view_0: This view shows the cylindrical bollard with prominent horizontal and vertical block lines and some greenish weathering.
description_view_1: This side reveals fewer vertical joints and more continuous stone segments, with subtle green patches.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a mix of horizontal and vertical block lines, with a slightly more irregular pattern.
description_view_3: This view displays a mostly unbroken horizontal band with minimal vertical joints and some visible weathering.
